steps.title: Find which form shows the coefficient equal to f(1)
steps.content:
A coefficient equals $f(1)$ when the exponential part equals 1. Since $2^0 = 1$, we need the exponent to equal zero when $x = 1$. Check the exponent in each form at $x = 1$:
  • Choice A: exponent $(x+1) = 1+1 = 2$
  • Choice B: exponent $x = 1$
  • Choice C: exponent $(x-1) = 1-1 = 0$
  • Choice D: exponent $(x-2) = 1-2 = -1$
Only choice C has an exponent equal to 0 when $x = 1$.

steps.title: Evaluate f(1) for choice C
steps.content:
For $f(x) = 128(2)^{x-1}$:
$$f(1) = 128(2)^{1-1} = 128(2)^0 = 128 \cdot 1 = 128$$
The coefficient 128 shown in form C equals $f(1) = k$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
